channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: President Samia Emphasizes Protecting Ngorongoro Conservation Area for Future Generations
item.description: Tanzania President Dr Samia Suluhu Hassan emphasised the importance of conserving the Ngorongoro Conservation Area, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, during a meeting at Chamwino State House in Dodoma on ...

item.title: Fuel subsidy removal pushes more Nigerians into poverty, study shows
item.description: The removal of petrol subsidy by the Nigerian government, as announced by President Bola Tinubu on 29 May, 2023, don sharply push up prices for petrol and other goods, waka increase poverty levels acr...

item.title: FCCPC investigates airlines for overcharging passengers during festive season
item.description: The Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission (FCCPC) don yan say some airlines wey dem catch dey increase ticket price anyhow during 2025 Christmas and New Year period fit face sanctions...

item.title: Satirical cartoon highlights absurdities in Australian politics
item.description: The article features a satirical cartoon by First Dog on the Moon, poking fun at Australian politics, particularly the National Party and figures like Matt Canavan. The cartoon exaggerates political b...

item.title: Women Employees Constitute Nearly Half of Workforce at VOC Port’s Berth 9 Terminal
item.description: At V.O. Chidambaranar Port’s Berth 9 in Thoothukudi, women have made remarkable strides in a traditionally male-dominated industry. Operating under a Public-Private Partnership model, Berth 9 has ensu...

item.title: R55,000 Raised to Support Boy Bullied at School Over Pie
item.description: A disturbing incident at Thembalethu Primary School in the Eastern Cape, where a young boy was embarrassed for not being able to afford a school pie, sparked nationwide outrage and compassion. The vid...

item.title: Majority of Filipinos Support Public Disclosure of China’s Activities in West Philippine Sea
item.description: A recent Pulse Asia survey shows that a significant majority of Filipinos back the continued public disclosure of China's actions against Philippine vessels and fishermen in the West Philippine Sea (W...

item.title: Los Angeles man sentenced for shooting that struck LAPD helicopter tail
item.description: A South Los Angeles man, Douglass Byers, 62, was convicted of firing a rifle shot that pierced the tail of a Los Angeles Police Department helicopter in August 2025, an act prosecutors said nearly cau...

item.title: Researchers uncover key nuclear processes in the formation of gold
item.description: A team of nuclear physicists at the University of Tennessee has made significant progress in understanding how heavy elements like gold are formed during violent cosmic events. Using the rare isotope ...

item.title: 15-year-old remanded in custody after school stabbing in Norfolk
item.description: A 15-year-old boy has been formally charged with attempted murder and possession of a knife on school premises following a stabbing incident at Thorpe St Andrew School near Norwich. The incident occur...

item.title: Lok Sabha adjourns till noon due to Opposition protests over LPG price hike and supply issues
item.description: On Friday, the Lok Sabha proceedings were adjourned till 12 noon following protests by Opposition members concerning the LPG supply situation and recent price hikes. Speaker Om Birla cautioned the Opp...

item.title: Six Police Officers Killed in Bettani Blast in Lakki Marwat, Security Heightened
item.description: In Lakki Marwat's Bettani tribal area, a tragic explosion near a police mobile vehicle led to the martyrdom of six police officers and injuries to one more, according to the official police spokespers...

item.title: Exploring Lakshadweep’s Traditional Cuisine and Signature Island Dishes
item.description: Lakshadweep’s cuisine is a fascinating reflection of the islands’ history, geography, and cultural connections. The first inhabited islands, including Andrott, Kavaratti, Kalpeni, Amini, and Agatti, w...

item.title: Philippine Unemployment Rises to 5.8% in January 2026 Due to Agriculture Job Losses
item.description: The Philippine unemployment rate climbed to 5.8% in January 2026, the highest since June 2022, as the agriculture and forestry sectors lost around 1.42 million jobs, according to the Philippine Statis...

item.title: Jeremy Hansen's Parents Visit Ingersoll High to Celebrate Students Following His Moon Mission
item.description: At Ingersoll District Collegiate Institute, a simple lunchtime Lego project evolved into the Artemis II Explorers Club, a student group dedicated to following Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen's upcomi...

item.title: Netanyahu Criticizes Iran’s New Supreme Leader Amid Ongoing Tensions
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu don yarn say Iran new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, na just puppet of the Revolutionary Guards. He talk am for press conference as tensions dey rise betwe...

item.title: Muhimbili Marks World Kidney Day After Reaching 100 Successful Kidney Transplants
item.description: The Muhimbili National Hospital (MNH) in Dar es Salaam celebrated World Kidney Day on March 12, 2026, marking an important milestone since the hospital first began offering kidney transplant services ...

item.title: Karnataka Government Prioritises LPG Supply for Hospitals, Hostels and Residential Schools Amid Shortage
item.description: On 13th March 2026, Karnataka's Minister for Food and Civil Supplies, K.H. Muniyappa, informed the Legislative Council that commercial LPG cylinders will be prioritised for hospitals, hostels, and res...

item.title: Rising UK energy costs are driven by global gas prices, not renewables
item.description: UK energy prices have surged, primarily due to global fossil gas prices, exacerbated by geopolitical tensions such as the conflict involving Iran. Contrary to claims from right-wing politicians, think...

item.title: Potential Fuel Shortages Could Hit Australian Economy Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: Australia could experience significant economic challenges if tensions in the Middle East disrupt fuel supplies. Iran’s recent attacks on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz using drones and explosive-lad...
